topStories.title: Watch: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei funeral: thousands gather in holy city of Qom
topStories.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets of the Iranian holy city of Qom on July 7, during the fourth day of the funeral for late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The remains of Khamenei are lying in state at the Jamkaran Mosque in Qom, a holy city that houses Shia Islam’s most influential seminaries and shrines.

topStories.title: World Cup after Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar: What remains in Kolkata?

It is saying goodbye to the players who shaped its arguments, allegiances and memories for nearly three decades.

topStories.title: In Karur, where there was no way out
topStories.summary: On September 27, thousands of people gathered in Karur, Tamil Nadu, to attend a rally by actor-turned-politician Vijay. As the chief of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam party arrived hours late, the crowd swelled and surged towards his vehicle, triggering a stampede that killed 41 people and injured over 100. C. Jaisankar and Nacchinarkkiniyan M. report on the tragedy, caused by many factors, including poor planning and alleged security lapses

topStories.title: Global Market: Britain's bond market poses early fiscal test for Andy Burnham
topStories.summary: Britain's government bond market is expected to be a key test for Andy Burnham's economic credibility as investors scrutinise his commitment to fiscal discipline amid mounting spending pressures. While easing bond yields have offered some relief, analysts say persistent inflation risks and rising debt servicing costs will keep markets focused on the UK's fiscal outlook.

topStories.title: TCS, Infosys and other Indian IT stocks rise up to 4% after AI worries trigger Kospi selloff
topStories.summary: Indian IT stocks climbed despite a sharp sell-off in Asian chipmakers, as investors positioned for Q1 earnings.  Indian IT stocks have had a rough ride in recent months because investors worry that AI could hurt billing growth, reduce manpower-linked revenue and force companies to pass productivity benefits to clients. With the sector having lost over Rs 17 lakh crore in market value, upcoming earnings and management commentary could determine whether the recovery has legs.

world.title: Watch: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India | Explained
world.summary: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

business.title: Global Market: Britain's bond market poses early fiscal test for Andy Burnham
business.summary: Britain's government bond market is expected to be a key test for Andy Burnham's economic credibility as investors scrutinise his commitment to fiscal discipline amid mounting spending pressures. While easing bond yields have offered some relief, analysts say persistent inflation risks and rising debt servicing costs will keep markets focused on the UK's fiscal outlook.

business.title: TCS, Infosys and other Indian IT stocks rise up to 4% after AI worries trigger Kospi selloff
business.summary: Indian IT stocks climbed despite a sharp sell-off in Asian chipmakers, as investors positioned for Q1 earnings.  Indian IT stocks have had a rough ride in recent months because investors worry that AI could hurt billing growth, reduce manpower-linked revenue and force companies to pass productivity benefits to clients. With the sector having lost over Rs 17 lakh crore in market value, upcoming earnings and management commentary could determine whether the recovery has legs.

business.title: NCLAT upholds insolvency proceedings against Farm2Energy
business.summary: A three-member bench dismissed the appeal filed by Sukhbir Singh, a director of the suspended board, and rejected his contentions of a pre-existing dispute and the existence of no 'valid operational debt'.

business.title: MF Picks: 13 stocks surge up to 230% in just 3 months; 3 turn multibaggers

business.title: Landslide in Wayanad tunnel project, several feared trapped
business.summary: The landslide occurred at Kalladi, where work on a road tunnel project connecting Malappuram and Wayanad districts is underway

business.title: Info Edge shares surge 11% after Q1FY27 billings rise 14% YoY
business.summary: Info Edge shares rose sharply after the company reported a 14.4% year-on-year rise in Q1FY27 standalone billings, led by strong growth in its recruitment and real estate businesses. Investor sentiment was also supported by the Coding Ninjas acquisition and additional commitment to its startup investment fund.

business.title: Global Market: Asian refiners turn to cheaper Gulf oil despite Saudi price cuts
business.summary: Saudi Arabia significantly reduced crude oil prices for Asian buyers this month. Rival Gulf producers are offering better value and deeper discounts to attract customers. Higher transportation costs from within the Persian Gulf also reduce Saudi crude's appeal. Weak demand across Asia, especially in China, further impacts Saudi oil sales.

business.title: Paras Defence, ideaForge, other defence stocks slide up to 5%. What’s triggering the selloff?
business.summary: Defence stocks declined up to 5% as investors booked profits after a sharp rally triggered by the Defence Acquisition Council’s approval of Rs 52,000 crore worth of acquisition proposals. Despite the correction, brokerages remained positive on the sector, citing strong order inflows and execution opportunities for companies such as HAL and Bharat Electronics.

business.title: Global oil market weathers Iran war shock, but reserve depletion raises future price risks
business.summary: The global oil market absorbed record supply disruptions during the Iran conflict without triggering a prolonged price shock. But analysts warn that depleted strategic reserves, damaged energy infrastructure and unresolved tensions around the Strait of Hormuz have left the market more vulnerable to future oil price spikes.

business.title: Ola Electric shares drop 9% in 3 days. What's spooking investors?
business.summary: Ola Electric Mobility shares fell over 2%, extending their three-day decline to 9%, after media reports said two vendors had filed fresh insolvency pleas against its subsidiary over alleged unpaid dues of more than Rs 40 crore. The company also recently reported a sequential jump in Q1FY27 vehicle registrations.

business.title: $11 billion worth of IPO shares set for lock-in expiry over next 3 months. What shareholders should know
business.summary: Nearly 53 companies are set to see IPO lock-in expiries between July and September 2026, potentially unlocking shares worth about $11 billion, according to Nuvama. While not all eligible shares are expected to be sold, the expiries could weigh on stock prices in the near term.

business.title: Global Market: Beijing, Hong Kong unveil measures to boost Yuan, bond and gold markets
business.summary: Beijing and Hong Kong announced measures to boost financial connectivity and yuan internationalization. The southbound Bond Connect quota increased, and yuan liquidity support expanded significantly. Hong Kong launched new gold clearing infrastructure and revived gold futures trading. Authorities are also developing electronic platforms for bond and foreign exchange trading. These initiatives aim to reinforce Hong Kong's role as a global financial hub.

business.title: NSE IPO: 5 reasons Jefferies believes the exchange stands out globally
business.summary: As NSE nears its IPO, Jefferies says its dominant market position and strong profitability set it apart from global peers.The brokerage expects the listing to attract significant investor interest.

business.title: ED searches premises in TMC bank accounts’ alleged ₹150-crore suspect funds case
business.summary: Investigation targets premises linked to Carewell Aviation and a purported electoral trust over suspected financial transactions.

business.title: Warren Buffett's successor puts record $400 billion cash pile to work. What's he buying?
business.summary: Greg Abel has started deploying Berkshire Hathaway's record cash pile after taking over as CEO from Warren Buffett. With nearly $400 billion in cash reserves, Berkshire recently invested $10 billion in Alphabet through a private placement, making it one of the conglomerate's largest holdings. The move marks a shift as Abel charts his own investment strategy.

business.title: Forget rockets: Morgan Stanley sets $300 SpaceX target based on a secret AI weapon
business.summary: Morgan Stanley has initiated coverage on SpaceX with an Overweight rating and a $300 target price, arguing its biggest opportunity lies beyond rockets. The brokerage believes SpaceX's vertically integrated AI infrastructure, combining terrestrial and orbital compute, could transform it into one of the world's most powerful AI infrastructure platforms.

business.title: Karur stampede case: SC refuses to entertain DMK’s plea on TN ministers influencing witnesses

business.title: Global Market: Japan's Nikkei falls 2% as Samsung slump sparks chipmaker selloff
business.summary: Japan's Nikkei share average closed two percent lower on Tuesday. Semiconductor heavyweights fell significantly after South Korea's Samsung Electronics tumbled. The broader Topix index also ended lower while value stocks saw buying interest. Investors booked profits in AI stocks and purchased undervalued companies. Japanese banking shares and Toyota Motor experienced gains during the session.

business.title: Global Market: Japanese bond yields retreat after strong demand at 30-year debt auction
business.summary: Japanese government bond yields fell Tuesday after a successful auction of long-term debt.  The benchmark ten-year yield retreated from multi-decade highs, easing investor concerns.  Longer-dated bonds saw steeper declines following robust demand for securities.  This auction offered relief after recent yield increases driven by inflation worries.  Market participants continue monitoring inflation and fiscal policy for future yield movements.

business.title: Did this Ashish Kacholia-backed multibagger stock really crash 81% in one day? Here’s how the bonus math works

In reality, the share price saw a notable increase after accounting for the bonus shares. This bonus issue marks the company's first distribution of free shares. Ace investor Ashish Kacholia holds a substantial stake in the cable maker.

business.title: Explained: Why did Samsung shares crash 10% even after tech giant forecast 19x jump in profit
business.summary: Samsung Electronics shares plunged 10% despite forecasting a 19-fold jump in second-quarter profit, as investors worried the AI-driven memory chip boom may be peaking. Analysts said strong earnings were already priced in, while concerns over slowing AI infrastructure spending triggered a sharp sell-off across semiconductor stocks.

business.title: Global Market: Japan reassures markets over BOJ independence, fiscal reforms

The government denied it was pressuring the Bank of Japan to keep interest rates low and reiterated support for central bank independence.

business.title: Global Market: Euro zone inflation risks persist despite oil price retreat, ECB's Schnabel warns

Her remarks suggest policymakers remain cautious on inflation, while another ECB official struck a more optimistic tone on energy prices.
